scope.summary:
Goal: Add an 'Outgoing Links' pane (egui + AccessKit, native) for the currently active rich document / loom block. The pane lists every link emanating FROM the active document — both wikilinks (`[[target]]` / `[[target|alias]]`) and transclusions (`![[target]]` embeds) — parsed from the document's DocModel / `content_json`. Entries are split into two buckets, Resolved and Unresolved (dangling), and every entry is clickable: clicking navigates to the link target via the shell navigation bus (the MT-030 navigation bus reused through the WP-011 command bus). This completes the Obsidian 'links triad' for the native knowledge surface: backlinks (MT-015 — links INTO this doc) + unlinked mentions (MT-024 — text mentions not yet linked) + outgoing links (this MT — links OUT of this doc).

File to CREATE:
  - `src/frontend/loom/outgoing_links_panel.rs` (~250-400 lines) — defines `OutgoingLinksPanel` egui widget struct plus the pure link-extraction function and the resolved/unresolved bucketing. This is the only new source file. It lives under `src/frontend/loom/` next to the existing `sidebar_panel.rs` (MT-024) so it can reuse the same backend client wiring and pane-registry registration patterns established there.

File to EDIT:
  - `src/frontend/loom/mod.rs` — add `pub mod outgoing_links_panel;` and export `OutgoingLinksPanel`; register the pane into the WP-011 pane registry under a stable pane id (e.g. `"loom.outgoing_links"`) so the shell can dock/show it.

State / data model:
  - `OutgoingLinksPanel { active_document_id: Option<String>, active_block_id: Option<String>, resolved: Vec<OutgoingLink>, unresolved: Vec<OutgoingLink>, loading: bool, error: Option<String> }`.
  - `OutgoingLink { raw: String, target_value: String, alias: Option<String>, kind: LinkKind, resolved_target_id: Option<String> }` where `LinkKind = Wikilink | Transclusion`.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. Link extraction is a PURE function: `pub fn extract_outgoing_links(content_json: &serde_json::Value) -> Vec<OutgoingLink>`. Walk the `content_json` DocModel tree (the same prosemirror-style JSON the rich editor stores). Reuse the MT-015 wikilink parser — DO NOT re-implement regex parsing here. Import the parser exposed by the MT-015 wikilinks module (`src/frontend/rich_editor/wikilinks/**`); call its parse entrypoint over text nodes and any dedicated wikilink/transclusion mark or node types. Port the React extraction logic from `app/src/components/RichDocumentView.tsx` (which collects links during render) and the parse semantics from `app/src/lib/editor/wikilink.ts` (`[[ ]]` vs `![[ ]]`, alias split on `|`, target normalization). De-duplicate identical (kind, target_value) pairs but keep first occurrence's alias.
  2. Resolution: for each extracted link, resolve `target_value` to a real id. Use the bound backend APIs (NONE new — reuse via MT-037 knowledge_documents binding and the loom block API): call `GET /knowledge/documents/{document_id}` to read the active document's `content_json` (source of links), and resolve each wikilink/transclusion target against the loom block / document namespace via `GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/blocks/{block_id}` (resolve targets) — i.e. look up whether the target name maps to a live block/document. If resolution returns a live id, push to `resolved` with `resolved_target_id = Some(id)`; otherwise push to `unresolved` with `resolved_target_id = None`. Batch resolution where the loom search/lookup API allows; never block the egui frame — do resolution off the render path and store results in the panel struct (set `loading=true` while in flight).
  3. Rendering: `impl OutgoingLinksPanel { pub fn show(&mut self, ui: &mut egui::Ui, on_open: &mut dyn FnMut(NavTarget)) }`. Header label 'Outgoing Links'. If `loading`, show a spinner; if `error.is_some()`, show the error text. Render two collapsible sections: 'Resolved (N)' and 'Unresolved (N)'. Each resolved entry is an `egui` selectable/button row showing the alias-or-target text plus a small kind glyph (link vs embed); on click, invoke `on_open(NavTarget::block(resolved_target_id))` which routes through the shell navigation bus. Each unresolved entry renders dimmed (non-navigable target, but still clickable to offer 'create note' future hook — for THIS MT just fire `on_open(NavTarget::unresolved(target_value))` so the bus decides). If both buckets are empty, render the exact text 'No outgoing links' (no panic, no spinner).
  4. Navigation bus: do NOT create a new navigation channel. Reuse the MT-030 navigation bus / WP-011 command bus. The `on_open` closure passed into `show` is the seam the shell wires to the navigation bus so an outgoing-link click lands in the correct editor/pane (MT-030 interop edge). Keep `OutgoingLinksPanel` decoupled from the bus concrete type via the `FnMut(NavTarget)` callback.
  5. AccessKit: every resolved entry node gets `author_id = "outgoing.resolved.{resolved_target_id}"`, role = ListItem (or Button), action Press. Every unresolved entry gets `author_id = "outgoing.unresolved.{target_value}"`, role = ListItem. The two section containers get stable container ids (e.g. `"outgoing.section.resolved"`, `"outgoing.section.unresolved"`). Register all ids through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry, never ad-hoc, so swarm agents can address rows deterministically.
  6. Reuse WP-011 shell primitives: pane registry (register `loom.outgoing_links`), command bus (the navigation seam), and the AccessKit id registry (entry ids). Do not duplicate any of those substrates.

Backend API bound (REUSE ONLY — no new endpoints, no backend rewrite): `GET /knowledge/documents/{document_id}` (read `content_json` — the source of outgoing links) and `GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/blocks/{block_id}` (resolve link targets to live block ids). Both are existing handshake_core APIs already bound by MT-037 (knowledge documents) and the loom block client used by MT-024. If a needed resolution capability is genuinely absent from the existing API surface, STOP and raise a typed blocker (do not add or rewrite backend endpoints; PostgreSQL/EventLedger only, no SQLite anywhere).

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
  - MT-015 (wikilinks + transclusion parser) MUST be merged — this MT imports its parser; re-implementing parsing is forbidden.
  - MT-024 (loom sidebar pins/favorites/backlinks/unlinked) MUST be merged — provides the sibling `src/frontend/loom/sidebar_panel.rs` patterns and the loom backend client wiring this pane reuses.
  - MT-037 (knowledge documents API binding) MUST be merged — provides the typed `GET /knowledge/documents/{document_id}` client used to read `content_json`.
  - MT-030 navigation bus must exist (transitively via MT-024 / WP-011 shell) for the `on_open` seam.

Risk if missed: The Obsidian links triad (backlinks + outgoing + unlinked mentions) stays incomplete — users can see what links INTO a note (backlinks) and unlinked text mentions, but cannot see what a note links OUT to. This blocks MT-044 (the triad integration / parity proof) and breaks Obsidian navigation parity.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: `extract_outgoing_links` is a pure function over `content_json` and returns links split into resolved vs unresolved buckets — proven by `cargo test extract_outgoing_links` against a `content_json` containing one valid (`[[ExistingNote]]`) and one dangling (`[[DoesNotExist]]`) wikilink: asserts exactly one resolved and one unresolved entry.

AC-002: Both wikilinks (`[[ ]]`) and transclusions (`![[ ]]`) are extracted, with `LinkKind` correctly tagged and alias (`[[t|alias]]`) preserved — proven by unit test asserting kind and alias fields.

AC-003: Link parsing reuses the MT-015 wikilinks parser (imported from `src/frontend/rich_editor/wikilinks/**`) — no new regex/parser is defined in `outgoing_links_panel.rs` (proven by grep showing the import and absence of a duplicate parser).
AC-004: Clicking a resolved outgoing-link entry fires the `on_open` callback with the target id — proven by kittest harness asserting `on_open` invoked with the matching `resolved_target_id`.
AC-005: AccessKit nodes `outgoing.resolved.{id}` and `outgoing.unresolved.{value}` are present with correct roles — proven by kittest AccessKit tree dump.
AC-006: An empty document (no links) renders the literal text 'No outgoing links' and does not panic — proven by kittest.

AC-007: Navigation routes through the reused MT-030 navigation bus via the `on_open` seam; no new navigation channel is created (proven by grep showing the `FnMut(NavTarget)` seam and pane registration into the WP-011 pane registry).

AC-008: Backend access is read-only and uses only the existing bound APIs `GET /knowledge/documents/{document_id}` and `GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/blocks/{block_id}`; no `src/backend/**` files are modified and no SQLite is introduced.
AC-009: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend` (native frontend crate) passes with no failures across all outgoing-links tests; no panics.
proof_targets: PT-001: `cargo test extract_outgoing_links -- --nocapture` => returns one resolved + one unresolved bucket from a content_json with one valid + one dangling wikilink; GREEN.
PT-002: kittest harness: click a resolved outgoing-link entry, assert `on_open` fires with the target id; GREEN.
PT-003: kittest AccessKit tree dump: assert nodes `outgoing.resolved.{id}` and `outgoing.unresolved.{value}` present with correct roles.
PT-004: kittest: render an empty document, assert the literal 'No outgoing links' string is present and the test completes without panic.

PT-005: grep proof: `import` of MT-015 wikilinks parser present; no duplicate parser; pane registered as `loom.outgoing_links` in the WP-011 pane registry; `on_open: FnMut(NavTarget)` seam present (no new nav channel).
implementation_notes: Keep `extract_outgoing_links` pure and side-effect-free (no I/O, no async) so it is trivially unit-testable; resolution (which needs backend lookups) is a separate step that consumes the pure extraction output.
De-duplicate on (kind, normalized target_value) but preserve the first alias seen; Obsidian shows each distinct outgoing target once.

Resolution must run off the egui render path: set `loading=true`, perform the lookups async (or via the existing loom client's request mechanism used by MT-024 sidebar_panel.rs), then store `resolved`/`unresolved` on the struct; never block the frame thread.
Reuse the loom backend client wiring already present in `src/frontend/loom/sidebar_panel.rs` (MT-024) rather than instantiating a new HTTP client.
Pass navigation out via a `&mut dyn FnMut(NavTarget)` closure so the panel is decoupled from the concrete MT-030 navigation bus type; the shell wires the closure to the bus.

Register every AccessKit entry id through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry; entry ids must be stable across frames so swarm agents can address them (`outgoing.resolved.{id}`, `outgoing.unresolved.{value}`).

Unresolved (dangling) entries render dimmed but remain present and addressable; clicking them still fires `on_open` so the bus can decide a fallback (e.g. future create-note flow) — do not silently drop dangling links.
Match the section ordering and labels to the backlinks (MT-015) / unlinked-mentions (MT-024) panes for triad visual consistency.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/RichDocumentView.tsx — outgoing-link extraction during render (which nodes/marks carry links, how the component collects link targets from the document model)

app/src/lib/editor/wikilink.ts — wikilink parse semantics: `[[ ]]` vs `![[ ]]` (transclusion), alias split on `|`, target normalization; port semantics by reusing the MT-015 Rust parser, not by re-porting the regex
binds_backend_api: GET /knowledge/documents/{document_id} — read the active document's content_json (the source of outgoing links); existing handshake_core API bound by MT-037 (knowledge_documents.rs:load_document). REUSE ONLY.

GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/blocks/{block_id} — resolve each wikilink/transclusion target to a live block id (resolved vs dangling); existing loom block API reused via the MT-024 loom client. REUSE ONLY.
No new endpoints. If a resolution capability is missing from the existing surface, raise a typed blocker — do not add or rewrite backend endpoints. PostgreSQL/EventLedger only; no SQLite anywhere.

risks: RISK-001: Re-implementing a fresh wikilink/transclusion parser inside outgoing_links_panel.rs instead of reusing the MT-015 parser, causing parse drift between backlinks/outgoing and silent triad inconsistency.
RISK-002: Resolution lookups run on the egui render path, blocking the frame thread and freezing the UI on documents with many outgoing links.

RISK-003: A dangling/unresolved link is mis-bucketed as resolved (or vice versa) because target normalization differs from the resolver's lookup key, producing wrong navigation or broken-link false negatives.

RISK-004: AccessKit id collisions when two outgoing links share the same target_value (unresolved) or the same resolved_target_id appears twice without de-duplication, making swarm-addressable ids ambiguous.
RISK-005: Clicking an unresolved entry panics or no-ops silently because `resolved_target_id` is None and the `on_open` path assumes Some(id).
RISK-006: Empty-document path renders a spinner forever or panics on an empty/None content_json instead of showing 'No outgoing links'.
RISK-007: Scope creep into backend (adding a new resolution endpoint) or introducing SQLite for a local link cache, violating the PostgreSQL/EventLedger-only and no-backend-rewrite constraints.
RISK-008: Navigation creates a new ad-hoc channel instead of reusing the MT-030 navigation bus, fragmenting the shell navigation invariant.
minimum_controls: MC-001: Unit test asserts the parser is imported from the MT-015 wikilinks module; CI grep gate fails the MT if a second parser/regex is defined locally.
MC-002: Resolution must be off-render-path (async or via the existing loom client request mechanism); a test asserts `show()` never performs network I/O and renders the cached struct state only.
MC-003: Bucketing test covers one valid + one dangling wikilink AND a transclusion variant, asserting exact resolved/unresolved counts and kinds; normalization key is shared with the resolver.
MC-004: AccessKit ids are de-duplicated and derived from (resolved_target_id) / (normalized target_value); a test asserts no duplicate author_id in the tree dump.
MC-005: Unresolved-entry click path is covered by a test asserting `on_open(NavTarget::unresolved(value))` fires without panic when `resolved_target_id` is None.
MC-006: Empty-document kittest asserts literal 'No outgoing links' present, no spinner, no panic.
MC-007: Diff gate confirms no `src/backend/**` changes, no new endpoints, and no SQLite dependency added; backend access is read-only via the two bound GET APIs.
MC-008: Grep gate confirms navigation flows through the `FnMut(NavTarget)` seam wired to the MT-030 navigation bus and the pane is registered in the WP-011 pane registry (no new nav channel).
